body.en:
# Flipper Zero Review

Hands-on notes on the multi-tool for hackers and hardware enthusiasts!

![flipper-zero](/images/writing/flipper-zero.jpeg)

Before going into the review I want to share some gadgets that I bought for it:  
  
- [Wi-Fi Developer Board ESP32, (2.4Ghz Single Band, unfortunately)](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FT89TNCL)  
- [GPIO Expansion Board, 433MHz CC1101 & 2.4G NRF24 Module](https://www.amazon.com/GEEKEYES-CC1101-NRF24L01-Expansion-NRF24/dp/B0GH8NXQT3/ref=pd_bxgy_d_sccl_1/)
- [2 Silicon Cases for Flipper Zero](https://articulo.mercadolibre.com.co/MCO-1433777185-2-fundas-de-silicona-suave-para-flipper-zero-con-mosqueton-_JM?quantity=1&variation_id=180653243062)


### Disclaimer 
> This isn't a "professional" review or anything like that but rather an honest opinion on the device, therefore, I'm not going to dig too much into the **well-known** technical aspects of the Flipper itself.


## Do you really need it?

I bet **you don't!** and that's a harsh truth, we (devs/hackers/hardware enthusiast/IT guys) have a tendency to gather things like this around us just 'cause it's fun!, but deeply inside you, you know you don't really need another paperweight, if you're really into *pentesting*, it might actuallly be a very helpful tool to have as your EDC, you can for sure customize it with some automation scripts or ~~bad~~ USB scripts and other things like that but for those kind of things you'd want to stick to [USB Rubber Ducky](https://shop.hak5.org/collections/home2/products/usb-rubber-ducky), [Bash Bunny](https://shop.hak5.org/collections/home2/products/bash-bunny) or similar.

I personally have found it more useful for *WiFi hacking* than anything else and with a proper dual band (2.4GHz / 5GHz) ESP32 WiFi board you can easily customize it as a Marauder device and it comes in handy for WiFi pentesting work.


## A word on the add-ons

Quick honesty pass on the stuff I linked up top, because buying accessories for a device you *already* admitted you don't need is peak enthusiast behavior:

- **ESP32 WiFi board**: I cheaped out and grabbed a *single band* one, and *"unfortunately"* is doing a lot of heavy lifting in that sentence. It works! but if WiFi is your main reason for getting into this (it was mine), get a proper **dual band** board from day one so Marauder can actually breathe. Learn from my wallet.
- **CC1101 + NRF24 GPIO board**: zero regrets on this one. It's a whole rabbit hole of Sub-GHz and 2.4G experimentation, and half the *fun* of owning a Flipper lives right here once you outgrow the built-in radio.
- **Silicone cases**: look, it's a couple bucks of peace-of-mind with a carabiner clip. Buy it. Your Flipper **will** kiss the floor eventually and it's also worth to buy a simple cheap screen protector as well.


## What I have used it for? 

- Learn a ton about GPIO modules, RFID, Sub-GHz and hardware and electronics!
- **WiFi Hacking** (Of course, under personal and allowed networks *wink, wink*)
- **U2F**, I think this option gives you a ton of security over your accounts but has several single points of failure, for example, if you constantly change device firmware or if the SD Card bricks and you didn't have a backup, say goodbye to this 2FA method.
- Playing Tetris (It's very fun to play it in the Flipper, lol)
- **Remote**, it definitely works fine to be an AC or TV IR remote.


## Firmware: pick your poison

I hinted at this up in the U2F bit, but it earns its own paragraph: the **firmware** you run *is* the experience. Stock is rock-solid and perfectly fine if you just want a clean, well-behaved device. The **custom firmware** scene is where the extra apps, codes and toys live, it's tempting, it's fun, and every time you flash something new you're rolling the dice on your settings, your apps, and (as I mentioned) anything bound to the device like **U2F**.

My advice: pick a firmware, **back up your SD card**, and resist the urge to distro-hop your Flipper every weekend. The grass is rarely greener, and a bricked SD with no backup is a *very* dumb way to lose your 2FA.

I personally stick to [Momentum-Firmware GitHub Repository](https://github.com/Next-Flip/Momentum-Firmware) / [Momentum-Firmware Official Web](https://momentum-fw.dev/), it has a great community, ton of support and of course is open source. 


[Unleashed-Firmware GitHub Repository](https://github.com/DarkFlippers/unleashed-firmware) / [Unleashed-Firmware Official Web](https://flipperunleashed.com/) is totally worth a shot, it has a more wide community.

## Is it worth it?

If you came here for permission to buy one, I already told you: **you don't need it.** But "need" was never the point, right? As a **learning device** it's brilliant. I've picked up more about GPIO, RFID and Sub-GHz poking at this thing than from any tutorial, and that alone earned its spot in my EDC. As a **pentesting tool** it's a *nice-to-have* that punches above its weight for WiFi work (with the right board), but it won't replace dedicated gear like a Rubber Ducky or a proper Pineapple WiFi device when you're getting serious.

Where it lands for me: a fun, properly **educational toy** that occasionally moonlights as a useful tool and a fantastic **IR remote** for when the real one has vanished into the couch. If that sounds like a fair trade for the price, you'll have a blast. If you were hoping for the magic hack-anything wand from the internet videos… *that device doesn't exist*, and the Flipper never claimed to be it.

Just remember rule one: test on **your own stuff**, on **your own networks**. *Wink, wink* only gets you so far in a courtroom.
body.es:
# Reseña del Flipper Zero

Notas prácticas sobre la multiherramienta para hackers y entusiastas del hardware!
